title: Newt values schema
description: Values schema for the Newt Helm chart

additionalAnnotations.description: Additional annotations applied to all rendered resources.

additionalLabels.description: Additional labels applied to all rendered resources.

additionalServiceAnnotations.description: Additional annotations applied to external Service resources.

additionalServiceLabels.description: Additional labels applied to external Service resources.

deploymentAnnotations.description: Additional annotations applied to Deployment resources.

deploymentLabels.description: Additional labels applied to Deployment resources.

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables injected into all containers (global scope). Map format: KEY: "value"

path.description: Health file path checked by probes

image.description: Global container image configuration used by all chart components.

digest.description: Image digest (overrides tag when set). Format: sha256:<64-hex>.

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets (list of Kubernetes Secret references). Each entry should be an object like: { name: "my-secret" }

registry.description: Container image registry (e.g., docker.io, ghcr.io, registry.example.com).

tag.description: Image tag (defaults to Chart.appVersion if empty).

adminAddr.description: Metrics admin server address (NEWT_ADMIN_ADDR). Used for health checks and metrics endpoint.

annotations.description: Extra annotations added to metrics-related objects (e.g., Pods/Service depending on chart logic)

otel.description: OTEL exporter configuration (used when otlpEnabled=true) These are passed as environment variables to the container

exporterOtlpProtocol.description: OTLP exporter protocol (OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL) - grpc or http/proto

annotations.description: Extra annotations applied to the PodMonitor

honorLabels.description: Whether to honor labels from the scraped target

labels.description: Extra labels applied to the PodMonitor

metricRelabelings.description: Metric relabeling rules (Prometheus Operator schema; treated as opaque objects)

namespace.description: Namespace override for PodMonitor (empty = release namespace)

portName.description: Scrape port name (must match a named port on the Pod/Service endpoints)

relabelings.description: Relabeling rules (Prometheus Operator schema; treated as opaque objects)

port.description: Metrics endpoint port exposed by the workload container

portName.description: Optional Service port name (used when creating a Service)

labels.description: Extra labels applied to the PrometheusRule

namespace.description: Namespace override for PrometheusRule (empty = release namespace)

rules.description: Rule groups/rules (templated). Structure is PrometheusRule-compatible; treated as opaque objects.

enabled.description: Create a dedicated Service for metrics scraping

jobLabel.description: Job label override (if your Prometheus setup uses a custom jobLabel)

labels.description: Extra labels applied to the ServiceMonitor

metricRelabelings.description: Metric relabeling rules (Prometheus Operator schema; treated as opaque objects)

namespace.description: Namespace override for ServiceMonitor (empty = release namespace)

relabelings.description: Relabeling rules (Prometheus Operator schema; treated as opaque objects)

targetLabels.description: Labels to transfer from the target Service onto metrics

targetPortName.description: Optional container port name to target instead of numeric port

namespaceOverride.description: Override the namespace for rendered resources (defaults to .Release.Namespace).

enabled.description: Enable native WireGuard interface (requires privileged container)

defaultApp.description: ----------------------------------------------------------------------- defaultApp — Baseline application rules required for Newt operation

kubeApi.description: ----------------------------------------------------------------------- kubeApi — Optional Kubernetes API server egress (typically TCP 6443)

defaultMode.description: Controls how global policies are applied to instances:

enabled.description: Master switch controlling whether NetworkPolicies are rendered.

ruleSets.description: --------------------------------------------------------------------------- Named RuleSets  Each ruleSet must resemble a NetworkPolicySpec fragment: policyTypes: ["Ingress","Egress"] ingress: [...] egress: [...]  Example: ruleSets: allow-internet: policyTypes: ["Egress"] egress: - to: - ipBlock: { cidr: 0.0.0.0/0 } ports: - { port: 80, protocol: TCP } - { port: 443, protocol: TCP }

nodeSelector.description: Node selector constraints applied to all Pods.

defaultTraefikTarget.description: Default internal Traefik service target for NOTES output

podAnnotations.description: Additional annotations applied to Pod resources.

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum unavailable pods (mutually exclusive with minAvailable)

minAvailable.description: Minimum available pods (mutually exclusive with maxUnavailable)

podLabels.description: Additional labels applied to Pod resources.

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Number of old ReplicaSets/ControllerRevisions to retain.

securityContext.description: Container-level securityContext override SECURITY NOTE: By default (when nativeMode is disabled), Newt runs as non-root: runAsUser: 65534 runAsNonRoot: true allowPrivilegeEscalation: false readOnlyRootFilesystem: true capabilities.drop: ["ALL"]  When nativeMode.enabled=true OR useNativeInterface=true: runAsUser: 0 (root) allowPrivilegeEscalation: true privileged: true capabilities.add: [NET_ADMIN, SYS_MODULE]  IMPORTANT: Do NOT set capabilities.drop when running as root - this is invalid.

statefulsetAnnotations.description: Additional annotations applied to StatefulSet resources.

repository.description: Container image repository for Helm test jobs.

tolerations.description: Tolerations applied to all Pods. Each item must be a Kubernetes Toleration object: key, operator (Exists|Equal), value, effect (NoSchedule|PreferNoSchedule|NoExecute), tolerationSeconds

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Pod topology spread constraints (applied to all Pods)

updownScripts.description: Map of filename -> script content (mounted when updown.enabled=true)

pullPolicy.description: Image pull policy (fallback when global.image.imagePullPolicy not set)

tag.description: Image tag (ignored if digest is set)

newtInstances.description: List of Newt instances to deploy. Each instance can optionally override

acceptClients.description: Accept client connections for runtime only (ACCEPT_CLIENTS env). Does NOT create any Service; Service is controlled by newtInstances[x].service.enabled

allowGlobalOverride.description: Allow this instance to override global settings (image, logLevel, etc).

existingSecretName.description: Name of the existing Secret with endpoint/id/secret keys

keys.description: Key mappings used inside the existing Secret

endpointKey.description: Key name for the Pangolin endpoint (default: PANGOLIN_ENDPOINT)

idKey.description: Key name for the Newt ID (default: NEWT_ID)

secretKey.description: Key name for the Newt secret (default: NEWT_SECRET)

blueprintData.description: Blueprint file content (inline). When provided, creates a ConfigMap with this content. Use this for simple inline blueprints, or leave empty to reference an existing ConfigMap.

blueprintFile.description: Blueprint file path (BLUEPRINT_FILE env). Path to a Newt blueprint configuration file.

configFile.description: Optional config file path for Newt (CONFIG_FILE env)

disableClients.description: Disable client connections (DISABLE_CLIENTS env). When enabled, Newt does not accept incoming client connections.

dns.description: Optional DNS server address pushed to the client (leave empty to omit)

enabled.description: Mount the host's Docker socket into the pod

enforceNetworkValidation.description: Enforce Docker network validation when enabled

enforceHcCert.description: Enforce health check certificate validation (ENFORCE_HC_CERT env). When enabled, health checks must verify TLS certificates.

extraContainers.description: Additional sidecar containers to add to the pod

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables to inject into the container

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts to add (must be valid Kubernetes VolumeMount objects)

extraVolumes.description: Extra pod volumes to add (must be valid Kubernetes Volume objects)

generateAndSaveKeyTo.description: Optional path to save generated private key (GENERATE_AND_SAVE_KEY_TO env)

healthFile.description: Health file path used by liveness/readiness probes

hostNetwork.description: Enable host networking (useful with native mode)

hostPID.description: Enable sharing host PID namespace (rarely needed)

id.description: WARNING: Use auth.existingSecretName for production to avoid secrets in values

initContainers.description: Additional init containers to add to the pod

interface.description: WireGuard interface name in the pod

keepInterface.description: Keep the interface on shutdown (native mode)

lifecycle.description: Container lifecycle hooks (e.g., preStop for graceful WireGuard shutdown)

logLevel.description: Per-instance log level (falls back to global.logLevel when empty)

annotations.description: Override or add custom metrics annotations

rules.description: Array of rule groups/rules (processed as templates)

targetPortName.description: Optional container port name to target instead of number

certPath.description: In-container path to mount the PKCS12 file

p12Base64.description: Inline base64 content for client.p12 (not recommended for production)

pem.description: Split PEM certificate configuration (used when mode=pem)

caPath.description: In-container path to mount CA certificate(s) (ca.crt) - comma-separated list

clientCertPath.description: In-container path to mount client certificate (client.crt)

clientKeyPath.description: In-container path to mount client key (client.key)

secretName.description: Secret name containing client.crt, client.key, and optionally ca.crt
Secret name containing client.p12 (if empty and p12Base64 provided, a Secret is generated)

mtu.description: WireGuard interface MTU (typical cloud path MTU ~1380). Leave at 1280 unless tuning

components.description: Instance-local components that can be merged in (mode=merge) or used exclusively (mode=replace).

includeRuleSets.description: List of named global `global.networkPolicy.ruleSets` to include for this instance.

mode.description: Composition mode for this instance:  Typical examples: - "merge": global defaultApp + (instance enables dns) + (instance adds custom) - "replace": instance defines a fully custom policy set, independent of global

useGlobalComponents.description: Select which global components are used for this instance (when mode is inherit/merge).

custom.description: Include global custom component (if enabled globally).

defaultApp.description: Include global defaultApp component (if enabled globally).

dns.description: Include global dns component (if enabled globally).

kubeApi.description: Include global kubeApi component (if enabled globally).

noCloud.description: Disable cloud connectivity (NO_CLOUD env). When enabled, Newt operates without connecting to Pangolin cloud.

pingInterval.description: Optional ping interval (e.g., "3s"). Leave empty to use default

pingTimeout.description: Optional ping timeout (e.g., "5s"). Leave empty to use default

podDisruptionBudget.description: Pod Disruption Budget override for this instance

port.description: WireGuard UDP port (PORT env). Default is 51820.

replicas.description: Number of replicas for this instance (default: 1)

secret.description: Instance secret issued by Pangolin (WARNING: Use existingSecretName for production)

securityContext.description: Container-level securityContext override SECURITY NOTE: By default (when nativeMode is disabled), Newt runs as non-root. When nativeMode.enabled=true OR useNativeInterface=true, Newt runs as root with privileges. IMPORTANT: Do NOT set capabilities.drop when running as root - this is invalid.

enabled.description: Create a Service for this instance

enabledWhenAcceptClients.description: Create Service when acceptClients is true (alternative to service.enabled)

externalTrafficPolicy.description: External traffic policy (Cluster or Local). Empty = omit field

loadBalancerIP.description: Static loadBalancerIP (when type=LoadBalancer). Empty = omit field

loadBalancerSourceRanges.description: Source ranges to allow (when type=LoadBalancer). Empty = omit field

nodePorts.description: NodePorts for NodePort type (optional, set only to fix ports)

tester.description: NodePort to expose tester UDP (leave empty to let K8s assign one)

wg.description: NodePort to expose WireGuard UDP (leave empty to let K8s assign one)

enabled.description: Mount and execute a shared up/down script

fileName.description: filename for the single up/down script

mountPath.description: Container mount path for up/down script files

useCommandArgs.description: Use command/args instead of environment-variable configuration

useNativeInterface.description: Use native WireGuard kernel interface (requires nativeMode.enabled=true and privileged)

annotations.description: Additional annotations applied to RBAC resources.

clusterRole.description: Create ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding instead of namespaced Role/RoleBinding.

labels.description: Additional labels applied to RBAC resources.

annotations.description: Additional annotations applied to the ServiceAccount.

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Control automounting of the ServiceAccount token on Pods.

create.description: Create a dedicated ServiceAccount for this release.

name.description: ServiceAccount name. If empty and create=true, the chart generates a name. If create=false and empty, the "default" ServiceAccount is used.
